minimal_measurement_breaker: paired NET formation assay + serum NET degradation/clearance assay (same sample, time-aligned)
why_it_breaks_the_ambiguity: separates the production flow from the removal flow that a single stock convolves
clinical_relevance: distinguishes neutrophil-intrinsic overproduction from a host clearance/degradation defect
claim_ceiling: design requirement, not mechanism proven; not clinical utility

why_it_breaks_the_ambiguity: estimates a relaxation/clearance time-constant rather than a single snapshot
clinical_relevance: identifies whether persistence is production- or clearance-driven
claim_ceiling: descriptive dynamics, not a causal disease mechanism

indistinguishable_mechanisms: increased modification rate vs increased released/persistent modified material
minimal_measurement_breaker: PTM-rate or enzyme-activity assay (e.g., PAD4) + total released material
why_it_breaks_the_ambiguity: separates a modification process from accumulated modified material
clinical_relevance: avoids inferring PAD4 activity from PTM presence alone
claim_ceiling: PTM presence is not enzyme activity or rate

indistinguishable_mechanisms: executed NET formation vs priming / potential
minimal_measurement_breaker: co-measured functional NET-formation readout in the same cells
why_it_breaks_the_ambiguity: RNA classifies state/potential but does not measure execution
clinical_relevance: prevents treating a transcriptomic endotype as executed function
claim_ceiling: state architecture is not executed function

indistinguishable_mechanisms: biochemical constraint vs executed NET phenotype
minimal_measurement_breaker: paired metabolic + functional readout under a defined perturbation
why_it_breaks_the_ambiguity: separates a biochemical state from the executed effector output
clinical_relevance: links metabolic state to functional phenotype only when co-measured
claim_ceiling: metabolic state is not flux and not function

indistinguishable_mechanisms: DNase1 deficiency/inhibition vs anti-NET shielding vs macrophage clearance
minimal_measurement_breaker: DNase1 activity assay + anti-NET antibody assay + macrophage clearance assay
why_it_breaks_the_ambiguity: localises which removal mechanism is impaired
clinical_relevance: matters because impaired serum NET degradation has been reported in SLE subsets and associated with lupus nephritis and disease activity
claim_ceiling: clearance locus, not a proven disease driver
